senses.definition: partially dictatorial; having some but not full authority or control typical of a dictatorship
examples: The regime was semidictatorial, allowing limited public discussion but suppressing major opposition.
A semidictatorial system may enforce rules strictly in some areas while remaining relatively permissive in others.

senses.definition: in a way that resembles a dictatorship but only partially or to a limited extent
examples: The leader ruled semidictatorially, cracking down on dissent yet tolerating some controlled forums.
Policies were enforced semidictatorially, with heavy penalties for critics but occasional loopholes.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being partly dictatorial
examples: The semidictatorialness of the government limited civil freedoms.
Debates focused on the semidictatorialness of the regime and its impact on youth protests.

senses.definition: a partial or intermediate difference between two things
examples: The semidifference between the two models is subtle but important.
Researchers noted a semidifference in outcomes across groups.

senses.definition: partially digested; not fully broken down by digestion
examples: The semidigested food remained in the stomach longer than expected.
Semidigested material may indicate a digestion issue.

senses.definition: describing an animal’s stance where part of the paw is on the ground and the heel is elevated, between plantigrade and true digitigrade
examples: Cats are typically semidigitigrade, standing partly on their toes.
Some extinct mammals showed semidigitigrade locomotion.

senses.definition: a partial digression; a departure from the main topic that is only briefly explored
examples: The speaker offered a semidigression about his hometown before returning to the main topic.
Her semidigression added a personal touch to the lecture.

senses.definition: state of being partly dilapidated; partially ruined or in disrepair
examples: The old mansion showed semidilapidation after years of neglect.
A semidilapidation of the building was noticed during the survey.

senses.definition: a chemical or biological concept denoting a semidynamic or partial intermediate substance (context-dependent; may be specialized terminology)
examples: The lab studied semidine as a potential intermediate in the reaction.
Semidine levels were measured to assess progress.

senses.definition: quality of being somewhat dirty or untidy; a partial level of messiness
examples: The room had semidiness, with papers scattered but not overwhelming.
He tolerated semidiness in his workspace but preferred order.

senses.definition: partially direct; not fully direct in approach or authority
examples: The project followed a semidirect management style.
They used semidirect control to influence outcomes.

senses.definition: the quality of being semidirect; partial directness
examples: The semidirectness of the policy made its effects unclear.
Critics argued that semidirectness weakened accountability.

senses.definition: partially disabled; having some but not complete disability
examples: The semidisabled employee requested accommodations.
A semidisabled status can affect benefits eligibility.

senses.definition: a half-formed disk-shaped object or storage medium; a partial disk
examples: The file was saved on a semidisk before transfer.
Semidisks were used in older hardware as transitional media.

senses.definition: occurring halfway between two musical tones; not a standard interval
examples: The composer used a semiditone to create unusual tension.
Semiditone intervals can sound unsettling to listeners.

senses.definition: occurring twice daily; relating to a tide or cycle that happens every half a day
examples: The semidiurnal tide rises twice each day.
Semidiurnal patterns are common in coastal regions.
synonyms: bimonthly (note: not the same; use with care)

senses.definition: partially divided into sections or parts
examples: The semidivided plot had two main sections.
A semidivided opinion among the committee members emerged.

senses.definition: partly divine or possessing some but not full divine qualities
examples: In myths, a semidivine hero has mortal origins but extraordinary powers.
The character is treated as semidivine by followers.

senses.definition: a half division or partial separation into parts
examples: The semidivision of the project created two parallel tracks.
Semidivision allowed easy assignment of responsibilities.

senses.definition: tending to divide into parts; partially divisive
examples: The policy sparked semidivisive debate among the crowd.
Her semidivisive remarks divided the group.

senses.definition: in a way that tends to divide into parts or viewpoints
examples: The speaker spoke semidivisively, planting seeds of disagreement.
Debate proceeded semidivisively, with audiences polarized.

senses.definition: the quality of tending to divide opinions or groups; partial divisiveness
examples: The semidivisiveness of the proposal slowed progress.
There is a risk of semidivisiveness in the upcoming debate.

senses.definition: plural of semidocumentary; media works that are partly documentary in nature
examples: The exhibit includes several semidocumentaries.
Semidocumentaries blend fact with scripted scenes.

senses.definition: a work that is partly documentary in style or content
examples: The semidocumentary explored historical events with reenactments.
Viewers appreciated the semidocumentary's factual basis paired with narrative.

senses.definition: a shape with six and a half sides; technically a 12-sided polygon divided into a six-sided and a split side concept (semiclassical mathematics term)
examples: A semidodecagon can be constructed by modifying a regular dodecagon.
Geometric drawings sometimes use semidodecagons for illustrating symmetry.
